HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

Go to the documentation of this file.
1
8
9#include <iostream>
10#include <string>
11#include <thread>
12#include <vector>
13#include "CLParser.h"
14#include "GreenF.h"
15#include "JobParser.h"
16#include "SIEFormPMCHW.h"
17#include "SimJob.h"
18#include "SurfaceMesh.h"
19
39int main(int argc, char *argv[]) {
40 // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
41 // Defaults for job configuration
42 // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
43 std::string jobFile("input.job");
44 std::string outPrefix("");
45 bool help(false);
46 bool NeedsAccurate(false);
47 int solver(2);
48 int etm(1);
49 std::string tableFile("");
50 int threads(1);
51
52 // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
53 // Setup command line parsers
54 // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
55 std::vector<CLParser *> clParser;
56 clParser.push_back(new CLStringOption("-j", &jobFile));
57 clParser.push_back(new CLStringOption("-op", &outPrefix));
58 clParser.push_back(new CLOption<int>("-l", &solver));
59 clParser.push_back(new CLStringOption("-t", &tableFile));
60 clParser.push_back(new CLFlag("--help", &help));
61 clParser.push_back(new CLFlag("-a", &NeedsAccurate));
62 clParser.push_back(new CLOption<int>("-etm", &etm));
63 clParser.push_back(new CLOption<int>("-th", &threads));
64
65 // Parse command line and overwrite defaults
66 for (std::vector<CLParser *>::iterator pIter = clParser.begin();
67 pIter != clParser.end(); pIter++) {
68 (*pIter)->Parse(argc, argv);
69 }
70
71 // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
72 // Handle help flag
73 // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
74 if (help) {
75 std::cout << "HELIOS SIE simulation program" << std::endl;
76 std::cout << " usage: main [-j JOBFILE]" << std::endl;
77 std::cout << " defaults: -j input.job" << std::endl;
78 std::cout << " switches: -l [0,1,2]" << std::endl;
79 std::cout << " specify solver type:" << std::endl;
80 std::cout << " 0: direct solver" << std::endl;
81 std::cout << " 1: cgsqr " << std::endl;
82 std::cout << " 2: LU decomposition [default]"
83 << std::endl;
84 std::cout << " -op PREFIX" << std::endl;
85 std::cout << " specify prefix for output file(s)"
86 << std::endl;
87 std::cout << " -t look-up table file" << std::endl;
88 std::cout << " look-up table for error functions - not "
89 "used by default"
90 << std::endl;
91 std::cout << " -a high-accuracy integration for matrix and "
92 "vector elements"
93 << std::endl;
94 std::cout << " -etm [1,2,3,...]" << std::endl;
95 std::cout << " number of evaluation terms in periodic "
96 "simulation, default value is 1"
97 << std::endl;
98 std::cout << " -th [0,1,2,...]" << std::endl;
99 std::cout << " number of parallel threads to run, "
100 "default value is 1, 0 uses maximum threads"
101 << std::endl;
102 } else {
103 // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
104 // Parse job file to decide task
105 // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
106 JobParser J;
107 J.open(jobFile);
108 std::string JobType(J.readTag());
109 J.close();
110 if (J.getTag() == "conversion") { // Mesh conversion job
111 SurfaceMesh s;
112 return s.ConvertMeshFile(jobFile);
113 } else if (J.getTag() == "job") { // Simulation job
114 SimJob *job = new SimJob;
115 if (tableFile != "") job->LoadTableFromFile(tableFile);
116 if (NeedsAccurate) Domain::EnableAccurate();
117 if (NeedsAccurate) GreenF::EnableAccurate();
118 if (NeedsAccurate) IncidentField::EnableAccurate();
119 if (etm < 1) {
120 etm = 1;
121 }
122 GreenF::AssignEtm(etm);
123 if (threads < 1 or threads > std::thread::hardware_concurrency()) {
124 threads = std::thread::hardware_concurrency();
125 }
126 SIEFormPMCHW::AssignThreads(threads);
127 SimJob::AssignThreads(threads);
128
129 // Run the simulation
130 if ( job->Simulate(jobFile, solver, outPrefix) == 0) {
131 std::cout << "Simulation completed successfully." << std::endl;
132
133 // Run post-processing
134 SimJob *jobPP = new SimJob;
135 if (tableFile != "") job->LoadTableFromFile(tableFile);
136 if (NeedsAccurate) Domain::EnableAccurate();
137 if (NeedsAccurate) GreenF::EnableAccurate();
138 if (NeedsAccurate) IncidentField::EnableAccurate();
139 if (etm < 1) {
140 etm = 1;
141 }
142 GreenF::AssignEtm(etm);
143 if (threads < 1 or threads > std::thread::hardware_concurrency()) {
144 threads = std::thread::hardware_concurrency();
145 }
146 SIEFormPMCHW::AssignThreads(threads);
147 SimJob::AssignThreads(threads);
148 return jobPP->FieldEval(jobFile, "", "", 0, true);
149 } else {
150 std::cout << "Error during simulation." << std::endl;
151 return 1;
152 }
153 } else {
154 std::cout << "Error ! Unknown opening tag." << std::endl;
155 return 1;
156 }
157 }
158 return 0;
159}
160
CLParser.h
Minimal command-line argument parsing utilities.
GreenF.h
Abstract base class for electromagnetic Green's functions.
JobParser.h
Lightweight XML-like tag parser for the solver job file.
SIEFormPMCHW.h
Header file for the SIEFormPMCHW class.
SimJob.h
Orchestrates a full simulation run: parse job/subjobs, build the linear system, solve it,...
SurfaceMesh.h
Declaration of the SurfaceMesh class and mesh edge struct.
CLFlag
Boolean flag option with no argument.

CLOption
Typed option expecting a value (e.g., "-th 8", "-l 2").

CLStringOption
Option that captures a string argument.

Enable globally more accurate (and usually slower) evaluation.

Request high‑accuracy evaluation paths (global).

Enable high-accuracy integration for excitations.

Streaming, stack-based reader for simple tag-delimited job files.

Close the currently open file.

std::string readTag()
Read the next tag token and update the open-tag stack.

std::string getTag()
Return the current open tag (stack top), or empty string if none.

Configure how many threads to use for parallel assembly.

Evaluate fields on a set of points read from a file.

Set thread count used internally by SimJob.

int LoadTableFromFile(std::string fileName)
Load binary lookup table used by periodic Green's function acceleration.

int Simulate(std::string jobFile, int solverType, std::string outPrefix)
High-level entry: run a simulation for a given job file.
